findings.title: FINDINGS_BY_BOT and NOISY_RULES silently limited to 30 days
findings.description: FINDINGS_BY_BOT_QUERY and NOISY_RULES_QUERY now include `AND created_at >= TIMESTAMP_SUB(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)` which was not present before. This is required by `require_partition_filter = true` on the table, but it silently changes the dashboard from all-time to a 30-day window. Users expecting historical bot breakdown or long-tail noisy-rule data will see reduced coverage.

findings.title: publishReviewCompleted test omits json.event_type check
findings.description: The publishFinding test verifies event_type in both attributes AND json payload (line 228: `expect(call.json.event_type)`). The publishReviewCompleted test only checks attributes.event_type but never verifies that the JSON payload also contains event_type. Both publishers emit event_type in the JSON body; the test coverage is inconsistent.

findings.title: finding/review publishers skip validateAndPublish
findings.description: publishFinding and publishReviewCompleted call getPublisher() directly, bypassing validateAndPublish(). This skips Ajv schema validation entirely for finding and review payloads. While intentional per comments (the storage types diverged from $defs.Finding/$defs.Review), any future BQ schema drift will surface only as silently dead-lettered messages rather than a fast-fail publish-time error. PR events still go through validateAndPublish, creating a dual-path inconsistency.

findings.title: Partition filter comment does not cover PR_FUNNEL_QUERY
findings.description: The comment block at line 96 states "All four findings/runs queries below MUST include a predicate over their partition column". The PR_FUNNEL_QUERY (query 5) uses `opened_at` as its partition filter for `code_review_pull_requests` but is not mentioned in the comment which only covers findings/runs tables. Minor doc inconsistency.

findings.title: Breaking rename: repo_full_name → repository_full_name
findings.description: The PR event attributes rename `repo_full_name` to `repository_full_name`. This is a breaking wire-format change for any Pub/Sub subscription that filters on `attributes.repo_full_name`. If the BQ sink subscription was already expecting `repository_full_name` this is a fix, but any other subscriber (e.g. alerting, audit) still using the old name will silently stop matching messages.

findings.title: PR funnel undercounts historical first_review stage
findings.description: The PR_FUNNEL_QUERY changed from `first_review_at IS NOT NULL` to `stats.time_to_first_review_ms IS NOT NULL`. PR rows written before this fix used flat fields instead of the nested `stats` struct. Those old rows have `stats = NULL`, so the funnel's 'first_review' count will be 0 for all historical PRs. The dashboard will show a misleading drop in reviewed-PR counts until enough new data accumulates.

findings.title: Two divergent publish paths in same module
findings.description: publishFinding/publishReviewCompleted bypass validateAndPublish, manually constructing attributes and calling getPublisher() directly. publishPrEvent uses validateAndPublish which adds event_type to attributes and performs schema validation. Having two publish patterns in one module is a maintenance risk — if event_type handling changes in validateAndPublish, the manual path won't inherit the fix.

findings.title: PR lifecycle test skips json.event_type assertion
findings.description: The second describe block ('PR payload matches BigQuery schema') checks many json fields (schema_version, id, org_id, etc.) but never asserts json.event_type. The publishFinding test (line 228) explicitly verifies `call.json.event_type`. The PR events' json payloads all include event_type from buildPrEventPayload, so a consistent test would verify it.
